name: technical-writing
description: Transform raw notes or findings into polished technical documentation (README, ADR, runbook, API docs, or design doc).

raw_content.description: Raw notes, code comments, or findings to structure

project_context.description: Brief project context for tone and scope alignment

steps.template:
Step 2 — Write the document:

Template for {{doc_type}}:
- readme: Title, description, quick start, installation, usage, API, contributing
- adr: Title, status, context, decision, consequences
- runbook: Title, when to use, prerequisites, steps, troubleshooting, rollback
- api-docs: Endpoint, method, params, request/response examples, errors
- design-doc: Problem, goals, non-goals, proposed solution, alternatives, tradeoffs
- tutorial: Objective, prerequisites, step-by-step, verification, next steps

Apply:
- Clear headings and hierarchy
- Code blocks with language tags
- Concise sentences (active voice)
- Examples for every non-trivial concept

Step 3 — Quality check:
- All acronyms defined on first use
- No assumptions about reader's prior knowledge (for stated audience)
- Commands are copy-pasteable
- Links and cross-references are valid
- Consistent formatting throughout
- TOC if document exceeds 3 sections
